pending.body:
finding_summary: `try_reconcile_failure()` can reconcile a stale bound attempt after a newer durable attempt already exists. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1859-1864`, `persist_workflow_run_id()` intentionally never overwrites an existing binding, so later reruns/resumes can leave a task durably bound to an older run. The new failure path then derives `started_at` for that bound run by preferring any lineage row for the same `run_id` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1982-2042`) and never performs the newer-attempt checks that the `workflow_run_id == None` branch already does (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2056-2093`). A stale `run_failed` from the old attempt can therefore be treated as the current failure, incrementing retry counts or escalating to `NeedsOperator` while a newer attempt is the real owner of the bead.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs
remediation_hint: Apply the same durable-attempt validation in the `Some(bound_run_id)` branch that the `None` branch already performs, and do not let a terminal lineage row outrank a newer `RunResumed`/snapshot boundary when choosing `started_at`.

finding_summary: `reconcile_failure_sync()` only suppresses stale replays when the newer retry reused the same `run_id`. The special-case guard at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:181-207` checks `newer_same_run_attempt_exists(...)`, but if the stale replay targets `run-1` and the active retry is `run-2`, the code falls through to `record_bead_completion_with_disposition(...)` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:281-302`. In that state the lineage store has only the newer attempt open, so the replay turns into a hard reconciliation failure instead of a harmless no-op/repair. The CLI sync path already treats this situation as stale and returns without synthesizing lineage (`src/cli/run.rs:1203-1212`), so the failure handler is now less robust than the rest of the reconciliation logic.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs
remediation_hint: Generalize the stale-replay guard to handle any newer active attempt for the bead/project, not just same-`run_id` retries, and skip or repair the historical event instead of trying to finalize against the newer open row.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 9 -> Plan and Implement
